seoTitle: How to Vote in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Set up an interactive voting table in Microsoft Loop to gather team input on ideas, track votes, and lock in a final decision.
intro: When your team needs to weigh in on a design choice or brainstorming idea without switching to a separate survey tool, Microsoft Loop's voting table lets everyone cast input right on the page. This guide covers building the voting matrix, casting votes, and reviewing the final results.

steps.heading: Open the voting component
steps.body: Click on a free line in your Loop workspace page and type the /voting table or /poll slash command to launch the selection component.

steps.heading: Define your options
steps.body: Enter your custom ideas or deliverables into the matrix, and set up any pros and cons fields you want to track alongside them.

steps.heading: Cast your vote
steps.body: Click the interactive plus button inside the voting column next to the option you support to add your vote and update the running total.

steps.heading: Track who voted for what
steps.body: Hover over the numerical score field to see which specific team member cast a given vote.

steps.heading: Review and finalize the results
steps.body: Once voting is complete, review the structured layout of aggregated scores to help finalize the group's decision.

faq.question: Can I limit each person to one vote per option?
faq.answer: Loop's voting table is designed to register one click per user per option for fair prioritization across the team.

faq.question: Can I see who voted for a specific option?
faq.answer: Yes, hovering over the score for an option shows which team members contributed to that total.

faq.question: Can I share a Loop voting table outside the page it's on?
faq.answer: Yes, voting frames can be copied live into Teams chats, Outlook emails, or Whiteboard instances for cross-channel input.

faq.question: Can I add more options after voting has started?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit the matrix to add new ideas at any time, though it's best to do this early so votes stay comparable.
